channel.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
channel.description:
I'm sorry, I can't get the result I expect... :sleepy:
If I access the following endpoint I get all the data I need: https://wp.laotramirada.com.uy/wp-json/wc/v3/products
How do I add the endpoint to the state?
Thank you!!

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: vscopise
item.description:
Hi @luisherranz, I’ve update de WC Plugin in https://github.com/vscopise/pxe-wc-api-rest to include all the variations.

The endpoint would be
/wp-json/wp/v2/product/<product-id>/variations

It is also possible to obtain the individual variation:
/wp-json/wp/v2/product/<product-id>/variation/<variation-id>

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Ok, then I guess you need to do an additional request, yes.

Does the URL /wp-json/wp/v2/product/516/variation fetch all the variations?

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: vscopise
item.description:
Hi, @luisherranz, they are separated request, one fetch for the product and other for the variation

/wp-json/wp/v2/product/516

/wp-json/wp/v2/product/516/variation/529

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Uhm, interesting. The WP sites is not working right now, but are variations included if you use _embed=true in the request or you have to fetch them in a separate request?

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: vscopise
item.description:
Hi @mmczaplinski !
I guess I need a handler first to get all the variation’s product and then add them to the state

the endpoint could be:

product/${product.id}/variation/${variation}

2 parameters, how should it be?

After that in the Variation Component I should build the form based on the different variations

Read full topic

Are there any errors that you are facing?

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: vscopise
item.description:
Hi @mmczaplinski !

In this example you have a Typical variable product, with 2 variations: size and colour.

In the product endpoint the diferents variation id’s could be obtain and there’s another endpoint to obtain the variation itself.

I would like to build a form that allows choosing between the different variations, as you can see in the Woocommerce Storefront theme.

Thanks !!

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: mmczaplinski
item.description:
Hi @vscopise !

I’m sorry but what is your question exactly?

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: vscopise
item.description:
Hi @mmczaplinski !

I have a question that I would like to share:

To handle variable products I have a “variations” field:
https://wp.laotramirada.com.uy/wp-json/wp/v2/product/516

and I also have an endpoint to get each variation:
https://wp.laotramirada.com.uy/wp-json/wp/v2/product/516/variation/529

I would need to create a form to choose the desired variation and thus obtain its price, its description, its image, etc.

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: mmczaplinski
item.description:
Sounds great @vscopise, keep us updated!

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: 403page
item.description:
I just updated the code on my local. Absolutely AWESOME. Orders work perfectly! I just want to add Stripe to my test frontity build to actually grab the payment before sending the order to wordpress and it’s ready for a basic live woocommerce store.

This use case is genuinely exciting. Woocommerce is a monster for CPU with all its admin ajax, cart refresh fragments, uncached logged in user sessions, etc.

Offloading that intensive part of the store to Frontity would save someone thousands, even tens of thousands in hardware costs at scale.

Really incredible work @vscopise!

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: vscopise
item.description:
Hi, @mmczaplinski
Until now, its a proof of concept, and I am taking advantage to learn React and Frontity.
I think Woocommerce is an excellent opportunity to apply the framework.
Soon I will include news, such as variable products and different payment gateways

Read full topic

Would love to hear what problems you came across (if any) and how you managed to solve them

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: 403page
item.description:
Nice!
I’m just working on the create order bit. On submitting it I’m getting {“code”:“rest_no_route”,“message”:“No route was found matching the URL and request method”,“data”:{“status”:404}} for /wp-json/wp/v2/create-order. I’ve put your PXE Extends WC Api Rest plugin on the Wordpress side - but I guess I still need to write that specific endpoint in.

Read full topic

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Great work @vscopise!!

Out of curiousity: why did you end up using useEffect instead of afterCSR to do the fetches?

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: vscopise
item.description:
the solution was a handler to read the necessary products:

const HeaderCart = ({ state, actions }) => {
  ...
  
  useEffect(() => {
    if (state.frontity.platform === 'client') {
      if (0 === state.theme.cart.length) {
        const storedCart = localStorage.getItem(
          'frontity_wc_cart'
        );
        if (storedCart && storedCart.length !== 0) {
          state.theme.cart = JSON.parse(storedCart);
          state.theme.cart.map(cartItem => {
            actions.source.fetch(
              `/product/${cartItem.productId}`,
            );
          })
        };
      }
    }
  }, []);

and the handler:

export const productHandler = {
  name: 'product',
  priority: 10,
  pattern: '/product/:slug',
  func: async ({ route, params, state, libraries }) => {
    // 1. get product
    const response = await libraries.source.api.get({
      endpoint: `product/${params.slug}`,
    });
    // 2. add product to state
    const [product] = await libraries.source.populate({ response, state });
    // 3. add route to data
    Object.assign(state.source.data[route], { product });
  }
}

It is important to consider when the product is loaded in the state to be able to display it in the component

Thanks everybody !

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: vscopise
item.description:
Using localStorage now I be able to get the saved cart, in my connected component:

const HeaderCart = ({ state, actions }) => {
  ...

  if (state.frontity.platform === 'client') {
    if ( 0 === state.theme.cart.length ) {
      const storedCart = localStorage.getItem(
        'frontity_wc_cart'
      );

      if (storedCart && storedCart.length !== 0) {
        state.theme.cart = JSON.parse(storedCart);
        state.theme.cart.map(cartItem => {

          // here i need to fetch each product from the api...

        })
      };
    }
  }

Do I need a handler to get each product?

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: vscopise
item.description:
@luisherranz describes a handler to load a product, which is not necessary now, since I handle products like Custom Post Types.
Now I need to load the products from the shopping cart (which I charge from localStorage) at the start of the app, I have the product id(s) from the localStorage

Thks!

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: mmczaplinski
item.description:
As far as I can tell you should do it in the same way as desribed by @luisherranz in How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

Does it not work? What approaches have you tried? Do you have an errors that occur with your solution?

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?

item.title: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
item.dc:creator: vscopise
item.description:
I can save and get the cart from the localstorage:

const addToCart = ({ state, actions }) => ({ productId, quantity }) => {
  state.theme.cart.push({ productId, quantity });
  localStorage.setItem(
    'frontity_wc_cart',
    JSON.stringify(state.theme.cart)
  );
  actions.theme.calculateCartTotal();
}


...
actions: {
  theme: {
    afterCSR: ({ state, actions, libraries }) => {
      const cachedCart = window.localStorage.getItem('frontity_wc_cart');
      if (cachedCart && cachedCart.length !== 0) {
        state.theme.cart = JSON.parse(cachedCart);
        state.theme.cart.map(cartItem => {
          //here I need to read the details of the products of the cart 
          //and save them in the state
        });
      }
    }
  }

How can I get the details of the products stored in the cart?

Read full topic
item.source: How do I add woocommerce endpoint to the state?
